Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists woolly as Solyc02g080260.

Source_Species.reason: Paper studies tomato and states tomato cv. Micro-Tom was used as WT.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods identify the species as tomato; scientific name Solanum lycopersicum is standard and consistent with the paper context.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods: 'Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) cv. Micro-Tom was used as wildtype (WT)'.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments on gene function were performed in tomato.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify Solanum lycopersicum cv. Micro-Tom.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Micro-Tom is explicitly given as the tomato cultivar used.

Regulator_Type.reason: Summary and Results describe wo as an HD-ZIP IV transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Figure 1f and text describe wo as HD-ZIP IV with HD, ZIP, START and SAD domains.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported for wo in Results/Methods: EMS mutant, CRISPR knockout, F1 cross, qRT-PCR, promoter:GUS, RNA-seq, GC-MS, Y1H, biotin-IP, Y2H, BiFC, LCI, pull-down, dual-LUC, MeJA treatment, and spider mite assays.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper does not explicitly report nuclear localization for wo; interaction assays imply nuclear function but no localization experiment/result is shown.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state wo was highly expressed in type VI trichomes and young tissues including meristems, young leaves and young flowers, with low expression in mature tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same tomato system as source species; expression pattern is explicitly reported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: GC-MS data in Figure 2 identify reduced 2-carene, β-phellandrene, and α-humulene in wo mutants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Text describes monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes, which are terpenoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Type VI trichomes are identified as major terpene producers, and leaf/stem/fruit trichomes are discussed, but direct metabolite accumulation was measured from leaves rather than explicitly from type VI trichomes of leaves, stems, and fruits.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary and bioassays link terpene/trichome traits to chemical defense and improved resistance to spider mites.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show wo loss reduces terpenes, impairs JA-induced type VI trichome increase, and decreases spider mite resistance.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results support wo as required for JA-responsive TPS induction/terpene biosynthesis in type VI trichomes and for trichome development.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Paper repeatedly states wo regulates terpene biosynthesis and trichome development.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Y1H/biotin-IP support direct binding to TPS1/5/12 promoters; dual-LUC in wo mutant background supports activation together with SlMYC1; JAZ2 repression is shown by interaction/competition assays.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: TPS1, TPS5, and TPS12 are the specific TPS genes directly analyzed as wo targets.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Results show physical interaction of wo with SlMYC1 and JAZ2 by multiple assays.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Data support promoter binding plus cooperation with SlMYC1 for activation, and JAZ2-mediated competitive repression in JA signaling.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: MeJA treatment experiments and JAZ2 interaction establish JA signaling state as upstream input.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results/model describe a wo-SlMYC1 regulatory module on TPS promoters disrupted by JAZ2.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: 1 mM MeJA treatment and spider mite feeding assays are explicitly described.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Native wo promoter for GUS reporter is explicit, but '35S constitutive promoter for transactivation assays' is not specifically stated for wo constructs in Results; dual-LUC effectors used pGreen II 62SK, promoter details are not explicitly given there.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of wo clearly causes broad trichome defects and reduced defense; paper also notes overexpression phenotype was not established in this study.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq of young leaves using Illumina HiSeq 2500 is explicitly reported; no accession is provided in the paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible inference from the data, but the paper does not directly present wo as a breeding target or discuss engineering value in those terms.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five summary points are directly supported by Results and figures.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show W106R in HD domain abolishes promoter binding but not SlMYC1/JAZ2 interaction, and Discussion notes broader wo roles than SlMYC1.

corrections.reason: The paper does not explicitly report nuclear localization for wo; interaction assays imply nuclear function but no localization experiment/result is shown.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists SlMYC1 as Solyc08g005050.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify tomato as Solanum lycopersicum.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Micro-Tom is explicitly stated as the cultivar used.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional studies were performed in tomato.

Regulator_Type.reason: SlMYC1 is described as a MYC family basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state SlMYC1 is in the MYC2 subclade; MYC family bHLH TF.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed assays are reported for SlMYC1: CRISPR knockout, overexpression, promoter:GUS, qRT-PCR, GC-MS, Y2H, BiFC, LCI, pull-down, Y1H, dual-LUC, MeJA treatment, spider mite assays.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No direct subcellular localization experiment/result for SlMYC1 is reported.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: qRT-PCR and GUS show high expression in type VI trichomes, GUS in type VI/VII with weak other-tissue expression, and Figure S8E shows increased expression during glandular cell development.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same tomato system; pattern is directly reported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: GC-MS data show effects on 2-carene, β-phellandrene, and α-humulene in CR-SlMYC1 and OE-SlMYC1 lines.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Type VI trichomes are the relevant production site, but the specific statement 'of leaves, stems and fruits' is not directly shown for metabolite accumulation; terpenes were measured in leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Terpene increase in OE-SlMYC1 is linked with improved spider mite resistance.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support increased terpene accumulation and pest resistance upon overexpression, decreased terpenes and type VII-like defective trichome phenotype upon knockout, and requirement for JA-induced TPS expression.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Paper concludes SlMYC1 is a key JA-signaling modulator for terpene biosynthesis and has additional JA-independent morphogenetic roles.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results directly link SlMYC1 to terpene biosynthesis and glandular cell/trichome development.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: qRT-PCR/dual-LUC data support activation of TPS1/5/12 with wo, and JAZ2 repression of SlMYC1-driven activation.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: TPS1, TPS5 and TPS12 are the three TPS genes analyzed in this paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Results show interaction with wo and JAZ2; Y2H screen shows interactions with JAZ2, JAZ4, JAZ6, JAZ8, and JAZ10.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Data support activation via cooperation with wo and repression by JAZ proteins, especially JAZ2 competitive interference.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: MeJA treatment effects are tested, and JAZ repressors interacting with SlMYC1 are identified.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Paper explicitly proposes functional wo-SlMYC1 module on TPS promoters disrupted by JAZ2.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: MeJA and spider mite treatments are explicitly used.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state SlMYC1 overexpression used CaMV 35S; native SlMYC1 promoter used for GUS; dual-LUC effectors used coding sequences in pGreen II 62SK.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper shows overexpression increased terpenes without increasing type VI/VII trichome number; no growth penalty is reported.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper integrates SlMYC1 with prior JA-responsive transcriptome and uses qRT-PCR, but no omics dataset was generated specifically for SlMYC1 in this study.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Although phrased interpretively, it is directly supported that overexpression raised terpenes and improved spider mite resistance without increased trichome density; thus engineering relevance is reasonably supported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All six summary points are supported by the Results and Discussion.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion and Figure 7 support the conclusion that many VII-like trichomes are defective type VI trichomes and SlMYC1 mainly affects morphogenesis rather than initiation.

corrections.reason: No direct subcellular localization experiment/result for SlMYC1 is reported.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists JAZ2 as Solyc12g009220.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify tomato as Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional interaction assays concern tomato genes.

Regulator_Type.reason: JAZ proteins are described as major repressors of the JA signaling pathway; JAZ2 is treated as such.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: JAZ2 is explicitly part of the JAZ family repressors.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Expression profiling, Y2H, BiFC, LCI, pull-down, Y3H, dual-LUC repression, and competitive pull-down are all reported for JAZ2.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No direct localization assay for SlJAZ2 is presented.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure S7/text state JAZ1, JAZ2, JAZ3 and JAZ7 were highly expressed in type VI trichomes.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same tomato system; expression pattern is directly reported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: JAZ2 is mechanistically linked to regulation of TPS activation and terpene biosynthesis involving 2-carene, β-phellandrene, and α-humulene.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Paper specifically frames JAZ2 action in type VI trichomes, the site of terpene biosynthesis under study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The terpenes are discussed as chemical defense metabolites against herbivores.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Mechanistic repression of terpene biosynthesis by JAZ2 is supported in assays and model, but no direct JAZ2 mutant/overexpression phenotype was tested in this paper.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Within the mechanistic framework tested, JAZ2 is the shared repressor connecting JA signaling to the wo-SlMYC1 terpene module.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results/model support JA-responsive control of terpene biosynthesis via JAZ2.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Dual-LUC and competition assays support indirect suppression of TPS1/5/12 activation via repression of SlMYC1 and disruption of wo-SlMYC1 interaction.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: JAZ2 directly interacts with wo and SlMYC1, and thereby indirectly affects TPS1/5/12 activation.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Physical interaction with wo and SlMYC1 is shown by multiple assays.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression and competitive disruption of wo-SlMYC1 are directly tested.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: The model states JA induces JAZ2 degradation, but this degradation of JAZ2 itself was not directly tested in the paper; it is inferred from JA signaling logic.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results support that JAZ2 separately associates with wo and SlMYC1 and interferes with their module.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: MeJA treatment is central to the JA-signaling context examined.

Applied_Promoters.reason: JAZ2 was used in dual-LUC effector constructs, but the specific '35S constitutive promoter' for those JAZ2 constructs is not explicitly stated in the Results text.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Reduced defense is a logical inference from JAZ2 repression of terpene biosynthesis, but no direct JAZ2 phenotype was tested in this paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable extrapolation, but the paper does not directly discuss JAZ2 as an engineering target.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five listed points are supported, with the fifth explicitly presented as the paper's proposed model.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Y2H data show several JAZs interact with SlMYC1, but only JAZ2 is supported as the shared interactor with both wo and SlMYC1 in this study.

corrections.reason: No direct localization assay for SlJAZ2 is presented.
